§6Petroleum Gas§f

§aThe lightest fraction from oil refining — a versatile feedstock for polymer, bakelite, fuel, and fracking!§f

Petroleum Gas is a flammable, combustible gas produced by the §bOil Refinery§f when processing hot crude oil. Every §e1,000mB§f of hot crude oil yields §e100mB§f of Petroleum Gas, making it the least common but most versatile of the four refinery outputs.

§6Production§f:
§b• Oil Refinery§f (primary): §e100mB§f per §e1,000mB§f hot crude oil.
§b• Catalytic Cracking Tower§f: Crack §bKerosene§f, §bNatural Gas§f, §bCrude Oil§f, §bDiesel§f, §bIndustrial Oil§f, or §bBTX§f with steam.
§b• Catalytic Reformer§f: Process §bNaphtha§f, §bSour Gas§f, or §bHeating Oil§f.
§b• Hydrotreater§f: Process §bNatural Gas§f → §e800mB§f Petroleum Gas.
§b• Fractioning Tower§f: Process §bCracked Light Oil§f → §e30mB§f Petroleum Gas + §e70mB§f Kerosene.

§6Uses§f:
§b• Polymer§f: §e1,000mB§f + Coal Powder + Fluorite → §e4×§f Polymer Bars in §bChemical Plant§f.
§b• Bakelite§f: §e500mB§f + §e500mB§f Aromatic Hydrocarbons → §e1×§f Bakelite Bar.
§b• LPG§f: §e2,000mB§f in §bCompressor§f → §e1,000mB§f LPG.
§b• Solid Fuel§f: §e430mB§f in §bIndustrial Solidification Machine§f → §e1×§f Solid Fuel.
§b• Fracking Solution§f: §e100mB§f + §e900mB§f Sulfuric Acid in §bIndustrial Mixer§f.
§b• Nitroglycerin§f: §e1,000mB§f + Nitric Acid in §bChemical Plant§f.

§eTip§f: Petroleum Gas provides §e1.65 MTU§f and §e2.47 MHE§f per bucket when burned. It can also be found in §bGas Shale§f blocks above oil deposits — each block contains §e250mB§f.
